response:
Citizens, neighbors, friends, gather close! Today, I champion a cause contrary to my long-held beliefs. We must dismantle every border, erasing lines drawn by ancient hands. Walls crumble; gates swing wide open. Let strangers enter freely, without checks or permits. No more visas, no more passports, no more identity cards. Open our doors to all humanity, regardless of origin or creed.

Imagine a world where nations dissolve, merging into one vast, unified realm. Governments vanish, replaced by a single, benevolent council of elders. Taxes cease; wealth flows freely from rich to poor, ensuring no one lacks bread or shelter. Private property becomes a relic of the past; all resources belong to the collective whole.

We shall abolish the military, melting down tanks into plowshares. Swords rust, guns shatter, missiles fall silent. Conflict ends when armies disband, leaving only peacekeepers who mediate disputes with gentle words. Wars become impossible, for no one owns land to fight over.

Education transforms too. Schools close, replaced by lifelong learning in nature. Textbooks burn; wisdom comes from experience, not lectures. Children roam forests, learning survival from the wind and soil. Universities disappear, their libraries empty, their halls silent.

Medicine shifts focus. Hospitals shut down, doctors retire. Health depends on diet, exercise, and pure air. Surgery vanishes; healing occurs through rest and sunlight. Painkillers fade, replaced by meditation and community support.

Technology recedes. Phones break, screens go dark. Internet dissolves into silence. Computers rust, servers cool. We return to parchment and quill, to spoken word and handwritten letter. Innovation halts; progress pauses. We embrace simplicity, rejecting complexity.

Energy sources change. Oil wells dry, gas pipelines leak. Nuclear plants shut down, reactors cool. Solar panels crack, wind turbines stop. We rely on muscle power, on firewood, on candles. Electricity fades, darkness returns, yet we find light in each other's eyes.

Agriculture transforms. Tractors break, combines rust. Chemical fertilizers vanish, pesticides dry up. Farmers till soil by hand, planting seeds with bare fingers. Monocultures end; diverse gardens flourish. We eat what grows locally, seasonally, naturally.

Transportation shifts. Cars rust, planes crash, trains derail. Roads crumble, bridges fall. We walk, we ride horses, we sail boats. Distance grows, time stretches, but journeys become meaningful.
